title: Frontend Engineer
description: We're looking for a frontend engineer who loves building fast, beautiful interfaces. You'll work directly on the Threadzip marketplace and AI suite — shaping the experience for thousands of fabric industry professionals.
responsibilities: Build and maintain UI components across app.threadzip.com and pro.threadzip.com
Collaborate with design to implement pixel-perfect, accessible interfaces
Optimize for performance, Core Web Vitals, and mobile responsiveness
Integrate REST and GraphQL APIs with clean state management
requirements: Strong proficiency in React and TypeScript
Experience with modern CSS (custom properties, grid, animations)
Familiarity with Astro, Next.js, or similar frameworks
Understanding of web performance and accessibility fundamentals
Good communication and ability to work async
nice: Background in e-commerce or marketplace products

title: ML Engineer — Fabric AI
description: You'll work on the core AI models that power Threadzip Pro and Lab — fabric generation, swatch analysis, and property-based search. This is a high-impact role at the intersection of computer vision and the textile industry.
responsibilities: Train and fine-tune image generation models for fabric texture synthesis
Build and maintain the fabric analysis pipeline (composition, GSM, weave detection)
Develop the semantic search system for property-based fabric queries
Collaborate with product to translate industry requirements into model objectives
Monitor model performance and iterate on production deployments
requirements: Strong Python and experience with PyTorch or JAX
Hands-on experience with diffusion models or GANs for image generation
Familiarity with computer vision tasks (classification, segmentation)
Experience deploying ML models to production
Ability to work with domain-specific datasets and limited labeled data
nice: Knowledge of textile science or material properties
Published research or open-source ML contributions
